steps.title: Focus on Dido's reaction
steps.content: The passage emphasizes that Aeneas's story and presence strongly affect Dido. Images of flame, love, and words imprinted in her heart all show an intense emotional response.

steps.title: Reject unsupported purposes
steps.content: A invents skepticism about Aeneas's account. C overstates intellectual engagement when the passage mainly stresses emotion. D treats the hidden fire as a secret Dido worries Aeneas will discover, but the text presents it as her growing feeling. The purpose is to show how deeply Aeneas's story affects her. The answer is B.
